entries.definition:
ḍ-w-ʾ intensive light, to light up, to shine, to illuminate, to
beam, to enlighten. Of this root, two forms occur six times in the
Qurʾan: أضاء ʾaḍāʾa three times and ضياء ḍiyāʾ three times.

أضاء ʾaḍāʾa [v. IV] I [intrans.] to glow (24:35) يكاد زيتها يضيء ولو لم
تمسسه نار its oil almost glows, though no fire touches it II [trans.]
to illuminate, to light up, to shed light upon (2:17) فلمّا أضاءت ما حوله
when it lit up everything around him, God took away
their light.

ضياء ḍiyāʾ [v. n./n.] illuminating; illumination, brilliance,
radiance (10:5) هو الذي جعل الشمس ضياء والقمر نوراً He it is who made
the sun a shining radiance and the moon a light.
